mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.02 or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of months with declining FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating inflation above 2.5% annually, the Federal Reserve tightens policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 70% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 245.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and higher corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.01 or positive returns occur in less than 50% of months with rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annually, robust economic expansion supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses the 0.6% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment indicates a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a statistically significant positive return differential with a p-value above 0.10 over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During stagflationary regimes where industrial production contracts while inflation remains elevated, corporate input costs rise and output falls, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 yields positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of observations matching the stagflationary trigger conditions.
